summary: Evaluate Unreal Chaos as the default Unreal-integrated physics path and Jolt as the specialized authoritative raft/contact candidate. Both targets must run the same fixed-step fixtures and emit the same telemetry before either can own scoring-critical raft, crew, swimmer, pin, release, or rescue outcomes.

authoritative_baseline.reference: GeoClaw/C++ validated water snapshots and solver-neutral scenario packages
authoritative_baseline.presentation: Unreal consumes interpolated fixed-step states for visuals, audio, VR, UI, replay, and debug overlays
authoritative_baseline.high_fidelity_reference: Project Chrono remains a research/reference path for multibody, compliance, and FSI experiments

candidate_authority_scope: optional raft/contact authority only if fixed-step replay and telemetry gates pass

expected_strengths: fast rigid bodies, constraints, CCD, ragdolls, characters, and buoyancy helpers
candidate_authority_scope: scrape, bounce, pin, release, and flip thresholds
scoring-critical crew/swimmer bodies if Chaos is not deterministic enough
runtime_targets.integration_artifact: future native Jolt bridge using the same water query API and telemetry schema

shared_fixture_contract.water_source: recorded custom-water snapshot or deterministic flat/current field fixture
comparison_outputs: Unreal debug overlay capture for Chaos
global_pass_gates: Same fixture inputs, geometry, water snapshots, material coefficients, and fixed-step schedule for both runtimes.
No runtime may directly mutate custom water state during these fixtures.
Scoring-critical outcomes must be reproducible from replay telemetry.
A runtime cannot become authoritative for raft/contact gameplay unless it passes determinism, runtime, and qualitative outcome gates.

fixtures.title: Raft Hits Rock At Multiple Speeds And Angles
fixtures.purpose: Compare elastic rock impact, deflection, scrape, launch, and spin behavior.

setup.crew: 8 seated crew masses locked to seats
setup.geometry: single rounded rock collider plus flat downstream recovery water
setup.water: constant downstream current with sampled custom-water drag and buoyancy

pass_gates: No tunneling through rock at the tested speeds.
No solver explosion or visible jitter after impact.
Replay determinism hash matches across 3 repeated runs for the same runtime.
Qualitative outcome stays in the expected scrape, bounce, deflect, or brief-pin class.

fixtures.title: Raft Grounds On Shallow Shelf
fixtures.purpose: Compare inelastic grounding, pivot, scrape, friction, and release behavior.

setup.crew: 8 seated crew masses with optional high-side input disabled for baseline
setup.geometry: sloped shallow shelf collider aligned with bed elevation
setup.water: thin custom-water sheet over shelf with downstream current

pass_gates: Grounding contacts remain low-restitution and high-damping.
Raft can pivot or release without infinite stick unless the fixture expects a pin.
Bed penetration stays under the configured tolerance.
Replay determinism hash matches across 3 repeated runs for the same runtime.

fixtures.title: Raft Pins Then Releases
fixtures.purpose: Compare sustained pin state, force buildup, high-side counterplay hooks, and release transition.

setup.crew: 8 crew with scripted late high-side input in the release phase
setup.geometry: two offset rock colliders forming a wrap/pin pocket
setup.water: constant high-flow current pushing the raft into the pocket

pass_gates: Pin state is explicit and does not appear only as jittering contacts.
Release can be triggered by the scripted high-side timing in at least the on-time case.
Late high-side input produces a worse outcome than on-time input.
Runtime emits force and contact telemetry sufficient for replay/debug.

fixtures.title: Crew Falls Out And Becomes Swimmer
fixtures.purpose: Compare seated-to-ejected-to-swimmer transition, ragdoll/character handoff, and scoring-critical safety state.

setup.crew: one bow passenger at ejection threshold plus seven seated crew masses
setup.geometry: lateral hit and low obstacle triggering a roll impulse
setup.water: custom-water lateral force snapshot plus downstream swimmer drift

pass_gates: Gameplay state changes are explicit: seated, at-risk, falling/ejected, swimming.
Visual ragdoll may diverge aesthetically but the scoring-critical swimmer state remains replayable.
Swimmer does not penetrate raft, rock, or bank geometry.
Replay determinism hash matches for gameplay-state telemetry.

fixtures.title: Replay Determinism Over 1000 Fixed Steps
fixtures.purpose: Compare fixed-step repeatability under identical inputs and water/contact samples.

setup.crew: 8 crew with scripted paddle, brace, and high-side intents
setup.geometry: flat current, one shallow shelf, one rock, and one lateral impulse zone

pass_gates: Outcome and event sequence match across repeated runs.
Position/orientation drift stays within the fixture tolerance.
Runtime can export enough state to reproduce or explain any mismatch.
A failing runtime remains eligible for visual-only use but not authoritative scoring.

fixtures.title: Runtime Cost With Raft, Crew, Rocks, And Loose Props
fixtures.purpose: Compare desktop and VR CPU cost with representative contact and ragdoll load.

setup.crew: 8 crew bodies with visual ragdoll proxies available
setup.geometry: 50 rocks, 20 loose props, shallow bed shelf, and bank colliders
setup.water: custom-water snapshot with sampled buoyancy, drag, and contact inputs

pass_gates: Physics cost stays inside desktop and VR budget rows recorded in docs/physics-runtime-budgets.md.
Loose props cannot alter scoring-critical raft or swimmer outcomes unless converted to deterministic hazard events.
Runtime remains stable with all representative colliders active.
Debug telemetry can identify the dominant runtime cost.

current_decision.default: Use Chaos for Unreal-integrated visual physics and non-authoritative props while the evaluation is pending.
current_decision.authoritative_candidate: Evaluate Jolt against Chaos for the raft/contact/swimmer gameplay island.
current_decision.do_not_do_yet: Do not replace the custom C++ water solver, do not let Chaos or Jolt mutate authoritative water state, and do not select either runtime for scoring-critical contacts before the six shared fixtures pass.
